data.title: BlackBerry in charts: IoT revenue rises Y/Y while cybersecurity revenue drops in FQ3
Earnings Snapshot: BlackBerry FQ3 results top estimates, FY25 rev guidance misses consensus
Primis Financial reauthorizes stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Primis Financial (NASDAQ:FRST) said on Thursday that its board has approved a stock repurchase program for up to 740,600 of the outstanding shares of the company's common stock.
  • The stock repurchase program will begin on December 19, 2024, and conclude on December 19, 2025, subject to the earlier termination or extension of the stock repurchase program.
  • Under the program, the company may repurchase shares of common stock from time to time in open market purchases or privately negotiated transactions.
  • The company also announced today that, as expected, it received a decision from the Nasdaq Hearings Panel granting the company's request for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, and confirming that the company has regained compliance.

data.title: Crown Castle gains on report it's close to $8B sale of fiber unit to TPG
data.description:
Private equity firm TPG Inc. (TPG) is in advanced discussions to acquire Crown Castle's (NYSE:CCI) fiber unit for about $8 billion. Shares of Crown Castle rose 7% in after hours trading.

A deal for the fiber and wireless assets may happen within weeks, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Nothing has been finalized, and the talks could still falter.

A potential sale comes as activist investor Elliott Investment Management has been pushing Crown Castle (CCI) to make changes, including evaluating the fiber unit, since late last year.

Reuters reported in early October that fiber network owner Zayo Group and private equity firm TPG (TPG) were competing to purchase fiber and wireless assets of Crown Castle (CCI) in a deal that may be valued at about $10 billion.

Elliott revealed in a letter last November that it had taken a more than $2 billion stake in Crown Castle (CCI) and was pushing for board changes and other moves at the owner of wireless towers.

data.title: Trust Stamp files to sell 9.72M shares of Class A common stocks by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Trust Stamp (NASDAQ:IDAI) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 9.72 million shares of Class A common stocks.

  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Raytheon secures $282M U.S. Air Force contract
data.description:
• Raytheon (NYSE:RTX) has been awarded a $282.34M U.S. Air Force contract for Small Diameter Bomb Increment II, GBU-53/B Production Lot 11.
  • This contract provides for all up rounds and containers. Work will be performed in Tucson, Arizona, and is expected to be completed by March 19, 2029.
  • This contract was a sole source acquisition.
  • The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center, Eglin Air Force Base, Florida, is the contracting activity.

  • Filing

data.title: Kroger announces agreement for $5B accelerated share repurchase program
data.description:
• Kroger (NYSE:KR) on Thursday said it has entered into accelerated share repurchase agreements (ASR) to repurchase, in aggregate, $5 billion in shares of common stock.
  • The ASR agreements will be completed under Kroger's recently announced $7.5 billion share repurchase authorization.
  • Kroger is funding the share repurchases under the ASR agreements with existing cash on hand.
  • Under the ASR agreements, Kroger will pay $5 billion to the dealers and expects to receive an initial delivery of about 65.6 million shares of Kroger common stock on December 20, representing about 80% of the total number of shares of Kroger common stock that could be purchased with $5 billion at a price per share equal to the closing price of Kroger common shares on December 19, 2024.
  • Final settlement under the ASR agreements is expected to occur no later than the third fiscal quarter of Kroger's Fiscal 2025.
  • In addition to the ASR program, Kroger has $2.5 billion of capacity remaining under its share repurchase authorization for incremental share repurchases over time.
  • KR +1.14% after hours to $61.66.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FedEx to separate freight operations, posts mixed results and lowers forecast
data.description:
Parcel delivery giant FedEx (NYSE:FDX) said Thursday it has decided to pursue a tax-free separation of FedEx Freight, the business which handles large cargo, and create two independent publicly-listed companies, as part of its streamlining efforts.

Shares of the company rose more than 10% in after-hours trading on the NYSE. Shares of rival UPS (UPS) were up 1%.

“This is the right time to pursue a separation as we respond to the unique dynamics of the LTL market...Through this process, we will unlock value for our Freight business and position FedEx to create even greater value for stockholders, CEO Raj Subramaniam said in a press release.

Stifel analyst J. Bruce Chan had predicted that there was more than a 50% chance the company goes through with a separation. He believes the potential upside from the separation is significant and could add an incremental ~$100 to the stock.

According to data from Bloomberg Intelligence, the freight unit has an enterprise value of more than $30 billion.

FedEx Freight reported $9.4B in revenue this year and is expected to benefit from "a strong balance sheet," according to the company. No executives have been named who would be part of the new company.

Goldman Sachs is serving as the company's financial advisor for the separation, which is expected to be carried out in the next 18 months

Q2 Earnings

Separately, the company lowered its annual forecast and reported a topline miss but a bottom line beat in its second quarter earnings report.

The company now expects flat revenue this year, vs. a low single-digit percentage growth previously forecast.

Earnings per share is now expected in the $16.45 to $17.45 range ($16.95 midpoint) before certain adjustments vs. the $17.90 to $18.90 per share range previously forecast.

Adjusted EPS is now forecast to be around $19.00 and $20.00 per share ($19.50 midpoint) vs. the prior forecast of $20.00 to $21.00 per share. The consensus estimate is $19.48.

The company said results were negatively affected by lower-than-expected FedEx Freight revenue and profit, as weakness in U.S. industrial production led to lower demand in the less-than-truckload industry.

Weakness in freight unit was mostly offset by cost reduction benefits at Federal Express from DRIVE program initiatives and higher base yields at each transportation segment, the company said.

Net income for the quarter was $741M, or $3.03 per share, compared to $900M, or $3.55 per share, in the year-ago period.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the company earned $4.05, ahead of the Wall Street estimate by 12 cents.

Revenue fell about 1% to $22B and missed the consensus estimate of $22.15B.

For fiscal 2025, FedEx expects to buyback an additional $500M in stock for total of $2.5B.

More on FedEx

data.title: Ionis gets FDA approval for Tryngolza
data.description:
The FDA has approved Ionis Pharmaceuticals' (NASDAQ:IONS) drug Tryngolza for the treatment of familial chylomicronemia syndrome, or FCS.

The product, also known as olezarsen, was approved as an adjunct to diet to reduce triglycerides in adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome, according to the FDA’s approved drugs database.

The FDA had accepted the drug’s market application in June, with priority review and a target action date of Dec. 19.
